name: hDNAgr hematopoietic stem-cell aging registry
summary: Not-yet-recruiting randomized blinded placebo-controlled registry testing hDNAgr therapy versus placebo in adults aged 45 to 65, with safety as the primary endpoint and telomere, blood-count, CD34-positive progenitor, biological-age, SF-36, and inflammatory secondary endpoints.
population: Adults aged 45 to 65 years without serious decompensated chronic disease

trial_details.expected_results_window: The registry estimated primary completion on February 1, 2026 and study completion on March 1, 2026, but still listed the study as not yet recruiting with no posted results in the June 9, 2026 API view.
trial_details.horizon_note: This is planned trial-watch context, not evidence of benefit.
trial_details.why_it_matters: A result-bearing controlled hDNAgr study would be one of the few targeted human HSC-aging intervention records, but the current registry only affects source completeness and safety/watchlist framing.
